name: Human idea bank conflict: increase the hot-band B shared-memory padding from +8 to +16 elements

summary.name: Human idea bank conflict: increase the hot-band B shared-memory padding from +8 to +16 elements
summary.hypothesis: The vertical export rewrite was effectively neutral on the dominant kernel, which means the next clean experiment should stay on the operand-delivery side. The hot-band path still uses WMMA-style shared loads for B and currently inserts only one 16-byte skew between the two 64-column warp groups. A slightly larger padding offset can change how the second half of the 128-column shared tile maps into banks without changing tile shape, consumer order, or stage count. Because the hot-band shared footprint still has headroom under the 48 KiB budget, this is a controlled bank-conflict test.
summary.expected_bottleneck: Residual B-side shared-memory bank behavior in the hot-band WMMA/PTX consumer path.
summary.risk: Low to moderate. The change is very small and local, but it does increase shared-memory footprint slightly and may do nothing if the existing skew is already sufficient.
summary.stop_condition: Reject if shared memory rises without an accompanying hot-band/runtime gain, or if scoreboard / mio behavior gets worse.

notes: Build passed. Node A must measure this implementation next.
